summary.title: [CSSECDV] GRP 2 - Practical Ex 1 - Threat Model
summary.description: Threat Model for Coffee Ordering  Web App

data.name: Customer
data.description: displays the order confirmation and updated order status.

data.name: Barista
data.description: Barista selects an order and updates its status (e.g., from “Received” to “Preparing”).

data.name: Manager
data.description: 3.5 Manager displays the basic sales report on their web browser.

data.name: Administrator
data.description:
4.1. Administrator logs into the staff interface and navigates to the menu management section. 

4.7. Administrator receives confirmation of the new menu item being added.

data.name: Web Application Server
data.description:
receives the order details and performs input validation 
and business logic checks (e.g., item availability, valid customizations).

data.name: Submits Order Details
data.description:
selects items, customizes them, and submits the order details to the 
Web Application Server.

data.name: initiates payment request
data.description: sending tokenized payment information to the Payment Gateway (a Third-Party Service).

data.name: Success or Failure
data.description:
processes the payment and sends a transaction response 
(success/failure) back to the Web Application Server.

data.name: Order Confirmation & Status
data.description: Web Application Server sends an order confirmation and status update back to the Customer.

data.name: View incoming orders
data.description: Barista logs into the staff interface and requests to view incoming orders from the Web Application Server.

data.name: Sends Order List
data.description: Web Application Server sends the order list to the Barista for display.

data.name: Sends Updated Order Status
data.description: Barista sends the updated order status to the Web Application Server.

data.name: sends pending order data
data.description:
MongoDB Database retrieves the pending order data and sends it to the Web 
Application Server.

data.name: Success = 1
data.description: If payment is successful, the Web Application Server records the new order details and payment status in the MongoDB Database.

data.name: View Basic Sales Report
data.description: 3.1. Manager logs into the staff interface and requests to view basic sales reports from the Web Application Server.

data.name: Queries for Aggregated Sales Data
data.description: 3.2 Web Application Server authenticates and authorizes the Manager, then queries the MongoDB Database for aggregated sales data.

data.name: Sends Aggregated Sales
data.description: 3.3  MongoDB Database retrieves the requested sales data and sends it back to the Web Application Server.

data.name: Sends a response on Sales data
data.description: 3,4 Web Application Server processes the sales data (e.g., calculates totals, formats for display) and sends it as a response to the Manager.

labels: Sends a response on Sales data

data.name: Sends New Menu Item Data
data.description: 4.4. Web Application Server sends the new menu item data to the MongoDB Database for creation.

data.name: Sends Confirmation
data.description: 4.6. Web Application Server sends a confirmation of the successful menu item addition back to the Administrator.

data.name: Inputs New Menu Item
data.description: 4.2 Administrator inputs details for a new menu item (name, description, price, etc.) and submits the information to the Web Application Server.

data.name: Trust Boundary
data.description:
Third-party services are external and partially trusted; communication channels are 
secured, but data received from them is validated.

data.name: Trust Boundary
data.description: The MongoDB database server trusts calls from the web application server’s identity
